stories.headline: Russian warship accused of firing warning shots towards yacht in Channel
stories.summary: Ministry of Defence says it is ‘investigating reports of incident’

stories.headline: Kyiv seeks license to produce Patriot systems for Ukraine and Europe
stories.summary: With Russia deliberately targeting Ukraine’s energy system each winter, Volodymyr Zelenskyy said at the G7 summit that he wants to bring the war to an end before the next cold season. Kyiv now holds a stronger position both on the battlefield and in its strikes deep in Russia.

stories.headline: FBI thwarted attack targeting UFC event at White House, director says
stories.summary: People have been taken into custody in connection with the alleged planned attack on the UFC event Sunday that President Donald Trump attended, FBI Director Kash Patel said.

stories.headline: Russia says yacht making 'dangerous approach' in Channel prompted warning shots
stories.summary: The incident comes two days after armed British forces boarded and detained a sanctioned tanker in the Channel that is suspected of being part of Russia’s shadow fleet.

stories.headline: Group planned to attack White House UFC event using snipers and drones, FBI says
stories.summary: In newly unsealed court filings, the government says the group allegedly expressed grievances about corruption, the Epstein files, and data centres.

stories.headline: Israel seizes power over occupied West Bank mosque from Palestinians
stories.summary: Hebron's mayor warns unilateral changes breach agreements, posing significant consequences for the region's stability.

stories.headline: UK investigating reports Russian warship fired warning shots near yacht in English Channel
stories.summary: BBC News understands the yacht had drifted towards the Admiral Grigorovich, a Russian frigate which has been operating in the Channel.

stories.headline: Russian warship fires warning shots near UK-registered yacht in Channel
stories.summary: BBC News understands the yacht had drifted towards the Admiral Grigorovich, a Russian frigate which has been operating in the Channel.

stories.headline: At Least 5 Charged in Alleged Plot to Attack White House During U.F.C. Event
stories.summary: Charging documents outlined an ambitious plan involving explosive-laden drones and rifles, but left less clear that the conspirators had the means to carry it out.

stories.headline: FBI says it foiled ‘planned attacks’ targeting White House UFC event
stories.summary: At least five people are in custody in an alleged plot that included 23 people, according to US media reports.

stories.headline: Cuba tourism collapses as US pressure campaign bites
stories.summary: The number of foreign visitors is down by 58% compared to last year, Cuban officials say, amid sanctions and an effective oil blockade.

stories.headline: Trump may release US-Iran deal before Friday, Vance says
stories.summary: The US vice-president says the agreement is "about a page and a half" and "very general", meaning many details will be worked out later.

stories.headline: Lukashenka apologises to Zelenskyy, calls him 'young and inexperienced'
stories.summary: Belarus leader Aliaksandr Lukashenka said that Belarus poses no threat to Ukraine as he apologised to Volodymyr Zelenskyy for his harsh comments in the past, while saying Ukraine’s president was “not a military man”.

stories.headline: Fujitsu chair resigns after ‘woman-related inappropriate conduct’
stories.summary: Japanese technology company at centre of Post Office IT scandal is negotiating settlement with UK government over faulty softwareBusiness live – latest updatesThe chair of Fujitsu, the Japanese technology firm at the centre of the Post Office IT scandal, has resigned after its board became aware of his “woman-related inappropriate conduct”.The company said on Tuesday that Hidenori Furuta had stepped down after two years in the role. Continue read

stories.headline: Half of world’s children exposed to at least three climate hazards, Unicef says
stories.summary: Almost every child, including those from high-income countries, is now exposed to at least one hazardHalf of the world’s children are exposed to at least three overlapping climate hazards threatening their health, education and survival, according to a Unicef report.Globally, children face increasing threats from heatwaves, storms, floods and droughts as the climate crisis worsens, with more than one billion facing at least three of these at once

stories.headline: Qatar renews mediation efforts for regional stability after US-Iran deal
stories.summary: Qatar says it is working to prevent a return to war, following the signing of the MoU between the US and Iran.

stories.headline: Israel went to war with Iran, but Netanyahu may be the loser
stories.summary: Critics say PM Benjamin Netanyahu's Iran war strengthened Tehran, strained US ties, and left Israel weaker.

stories.headline: Qatar 'cautiously optimistic' on US-Iran framework deal before Friday's signing
stories.summary: Qatar said the framework agreement to be signed on Friday was a first step rather than a final settlement, adding that it would take time to return to "business as usual" even once the Strait of Hormuz reopens. Trump separately praised Qatar's mediating role and its "great bravery".

stories.headline: Tehran selling deal with US as victory – but for Iranians it was necessity
stories.summary: For many Iranians, the question is not whether the deal means victory, but whether it lowers prices and reduces fear of another war.

stories.headline: Russian artist and Putin critic shot dead in Poland
stories.summary: Robert Kuzovkov, who used the pseudonym Semyon Skrepetsky, has been known for his caricatures of politicians including Vladimir Putin.

stories.headline: Iranian-Americans protest against Iran team at World Cup
stories.summary: The BBC’s Shaimaa Khalil was outside the Iran v New Zealand opening round match as protesters called for an end to Tehran’s clerical regime.

stories.headline: Iran deal presents political nightmare for Netanyahu
stories.summary: Donald Trump's ceasefire agreement with Iran leaves the Israeli PM trapped in a new political and security dilemma.

stories.headline: Stanford's Wallace Says US Recession Is Not a Certainty
stories.summary: Stanford Management Company CEO Rob Wallace discusses his views on the US economy, and the challenges of managing a $50 billion portfolio for Stanford University's endowment. He joins David Rubenstein on this week's episode of Bloomberg Wealth. The interview was recorded April 9 in Palo Alto, CA.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Finally, an interest rate reprieve – but a ceasefire in the Middle East doesn’t have the RBA popping champagne yet
stories.summary: Governor Michele Bullock delivers a strong message after the Reserve Bank holds the cash rate at 4.35%, ending a run of three risesRBA interest rates: Reserve Bank holds official cash rate at 4.35%Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastIt will take more than a ceasefire in the Middle East to prevent the Reserve Bank from hiking interest rates again.That was the strong message from the RBA governor, Michele Bullock, after the c

stories.headline: Trump slams Israeli attacks on Lebanon at G7 summit
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump slammed Israeli attacks on Lebanon at the G7 Summit.

stories.headline: Nearly all children globally exposed to at least one climate hazard: Report
stories.summary: Report highlights the growing threats posed by climate change and calls for the green transition to be accelerated.

stories.headline: Japan ride-hailing app Go races toward robotaxis after successful IPO
Bangladesh bets $3.2bn to avert a banking crisis but reforms elusive
Venezuela signs deal with US energy giant to rebuild power grid
stories.summary: The deal with General Electric is the latest sign of co-operation between US firms and Venezuela's interim government.

stories.headline: Hungary's MPs block return of Orbán, limiting rule of PM to eight years
stories.summary: The vote fulfils a promise by new PM Péter Magyar to stop his predecessor from becoming prime minister again.

stories.headline: India temporarily bans Telegram over exam paper leak concerns
stories.summary: The NEET exam had earlier been scrapped over allegations of a paper leak, sparking massive protests.

stories.headline: Japan raises interest rate to highest for 31 years
stories.summary: The Bank of Japan has been raising rates from near-zero since 2024.

stories.headline: The reclusive heirs in line for billions when Europe’s top tankmaker lists
stories.summary: Unlikely cast of characters who own half of KNDS set for a windfall as Berlin prepares to buy in at an up to €20bn valuation

stories.headline: Aluminum Rises as Traders Weigh Chinese Output, Mideast Supply
stories.summary: Aluminum edged higher as investors assessed the potential for long-term supply limitations despite booming Chinese production and the possible return of Middle East smelters to the global market.

stories.headline: SpaceX Set to Overtake Amazon in Value as It Soars for Third Day
stories.summary: SpaceX jumped for a third straight day on Tuesday, putting it on track to overtake Amazon.com Inc. in value and briefly making it the world’s fourth largest stock ahead of Microsoft Corp.

stories.headline: Sports as an Asset Class
stories.summary: "Fundamentally, sports is an asset class," said Dave Dase, global co-head of sports franchise in investment banking at Goldman Sachs. Dase discussed how the expanding sports market and increased liquidity are positively impacting the valuations and financial health of sports teams and leagues. He spoke on June 15 on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Philippines Set to Raise $2.5 Billion in Its Second Deal of Year
stories.summary: The Philippines is returning to the international bond market for a second time this year, seizing the opportunity to finance government spending as borrowing costs ease amid optimism about a potential US-Iran peace agreement.

stories.headline: Ethiopia Dollar Sales Over $2 Billion as Parallel Market Rate Surges
stories.summary: Ethiopia’s latest special auction has pushed dollar sales this year above the $2 billion mark, as authorities step up efforts to support the birr and narrow the gap between official and parallel-market exchange rates.

stories.headline: Binance Prepares for EU Exit With Local License in Limbo
stories.summary: Binance Holdings Ltd. is preparing to stop providing its services in the European Union, as a deadline to receive a license to operate in the bloc draws near and the crypto exchange has yet to receive approval from Greece’s financial regulator.

stories.headline: European Stocks Gain on Optimism Over Strait of Hormuz Reopening
stories.summary: European stocks edged higher, building on Monday’s record close, as the prospect of the Strait of Hormuz reopening later this week boosted appetite for risk among investors.

stories.headline: A Billion Dollars of Leveraged SpaceX Bets Hit ETFs in One Day
stories.summary: Leveraged bets on SpaceX generated more than $1 billion of trading volume on their first day, as investors rushed to amplify wagers on the biggest IPO in history, underscoring the extraordinary retail appetite surrounding Elon Musk’s newest public company.

stories.headline: Federal Judge Blocks Idaho Law Criminalizing Transgender Bathroom Use
stories.summary: Six transgender residents claimed that the bathroom ban, the most restrictive in the nation, violated their constitutional rights.

stories.headline: Claiming an Antifa Plot, U.S. Charges 15 in Minneapolis With Conspiracy
stories.summary: The 94-page conspiracy indictment was filed as federal prosecutors in Minnesota had trouble sustaining many criminal cases filed against Immigration and Customs Enforcement protesters.

stories.headline: What to Watch in Primary and Runoff Elections in Georgia, Alabama and Oklahoma
stories.summary: The top race of the day is in Georgia, where Republican voters will pick a nominee to challenge Senator Jon Ossoff, a Democrat.

stories.headline: France to ditch Palantir’s AI data tools in favour of domestic provider
stories.summary: Move to ChapsVision is to avoid ‘strategic dependencies’, says PM amid concern about reliance on US-controlled toolsFrance’s domestic intelligence service is to ditch AI data tools from the US tech company Palantir in favour of a domestic provider in an effort to avoid “strategic dependency”, the prime minister, Sébastien Lecornu, has said.“We must use our own AI models; we cannot accept new strategic dependencies in ‌the digital sphere,” Lecornu

stories.headline: Battery storage costs fall below gas-fired power plants for first time
India's Kaynes enters Japan's chip assembly market with local partners
Japan's Marubeni buys Texas gas producer EagleRidge Energy II
From oil to batteries, Chinese energy companies flag first-half windfalls
Central banks expect their gold reserves to rise as de-dollarization continues
SK's triple play and Japan's subsea strategy
China retail sales fall for first time since COVID lockdowns
Church of Sweden launches inquiry into PM's wife over unspecified ethical concerns
stories.summary: The Lutheran Church of Sweden, which has some 5.4 million members in a country of 10.6 million, formally separated from the Swedish state in 2000.

stories.headline: Trump aims to end Iran war but nuclear issue remains unresolved
stories.summary: President Trump says the US and Iran have agreed a deal to end the war but it is unclear what has been agreed on Iran's nuclear programme

stories.headline: Trump hosts UFC fights on White House lawn
stories.summary: The South Lawn event was meant to celebrate the nation’s 250th anniversary and the president's 80th birthday.

stories.headline: Trump sought to break Iran’s regime. He settled for reopening Hormuz.
stories.summary: The emerging agreement ends a costly war but leaves Iran’s leadership intact and its nuclear future still subject to negotiation.

stories.headline: Captain of Russian shadow fleet tanker intercepted in Channel charged
stories.summary: An Indian national is charged with contravening sanctions after the tanker was detained.

stories.headline: Unclear if US-Iran agreement will provide respite to Lebanon
stories.summary: Pakistan says the deal includes Lebanon, but recent ceasefires there have failed to take hold.

stories.headline: Tech Stocks Halt S&P 500 Rally While SpaceX Surges: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: A rally that sent the US stock benchmark to the brink of a record faltered ahead of the first Federal Reserve rate decision under Kevin Warsh, with a another slide in oil prices driving bond yields lower.

stories.headline: Oil Falls to Three-Month Low as US-Iran Deal Set to Add Supply
stories.summary: Oil headed for its longest run of declines this year as the US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz boosted expectations for a revival in supply, with leading Wall Street banks reducing their price forecasts and key market gauges tumbling.

stories.headline: What one country's experiment says about attempts to boost birth rates
stories.summary: Why did Hungary’s pronatalist approach deliver an early rise in births only then to fall back? And what lessons does it offer to other countries desperate to lift fertility?

stories.headline: Dublin-born Cape Verde star recruited on LinkedIn gets World Cup chance
stories.summary: They are one of the smallest countries to play in a World Cup, but Cape Verde are aiming high, with a Dublin-born defender who used to work in a bank among their ranks.

stories.headline: 'Spider-Man of Yemen' dies falling into volcanic crater
stories.summary: Al-Qaqa Ibn Antar had been attempting to climb its walls without safety equipment when he fell in, local authorities say.

stories.headline: South Korea's Starbucks to shut for staff history lesson after backlash
stories.summary: The chain was criticised for a "Tank Day" marketing ploy on the anniversary of a military massacre.

stories.headline: Dan Sullivan Namesake Is Disqualified From Alaska Senate Ballot
stories.summary: Republicans had accused Democrats of attempting to confuse voters by boosting a candidate with the same name as the incumbent Republican senator.

stories.headline: Argentinian activist who spent 50 years looking for disappeared son dies
stories.summary: Outpouring of public grief for Lidia ‘Taty’ Almeida, leader of group of mothers that has marched every week since 1977The human rights activist Lidia “Taty” Almeida – who spent more than half a century searching for her son after he was forcibly disappeared by Argentina’s military junta – has died aged 95, prompting a public outpouring of grief.Almeida, 95, was the president of the Mothers of Plaza de Mayo, made up of women who have marched aroun

stories.headline: US kills leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua gang in air strike, Trump says
stories.summary: Niño Guerrero was killed in a "swift and lethal kinetic strike," the US President wrote in a social media post.

stories.headline: Swiss voters reject 10 million population cap
stories.summary: Nearly 55% of participants voted against the proposal to cap population by cutting migration, results show.

stories.headline: UK and Japan agree £18bn investment deal
stories.summary: Japanese firms will spend billions on UK infrastructure and offshore wind, Downing Street says.

stories.headline: Russian families use AI to 'resurrect' loved ones killed in Ukraine
stories.summary: The highly controversial trend lies at the intersection of Russia's war on Ukraine, new AI technologies and grief.

stories.headline: Vincent's parents 'never say he's good enough' - so he turned to a middle-aged couple online
stories.summary: A niche group of content creators called "virtual parents" have become hugely popular among young people.

stories.headline: Watch: Fans celebrate Mexico's win in World Cup opening match
stories.summary: There were wild celebrations in host nation Mexico, as their team kicked off their World Cup campaign with a 2-0 win over South Africa.

stories.headline: Israeli tech firm accused of targeting First Minister in election
stories.summary: Accounts linked to tech firm BlackCore are alleged to have carried out 'digital interference operations' against John Swinney.

stories.headline: The value of SpaceX rockets on its stock-market debut
stories.summary: Elon Musk has become the world’s first trillionaire

stories.headline: American capitalism is run by millionaires, not billionaires
stories.summary: They hide in plain sight—and wield enormous power

stories.headline: Global brands ‘likely’ using mineral that funds rebels accused of atrocities in DRC, investigation finds
stories.summary: Amazon and Sony among firms that may have sourced coltan, used in phones, from supply chains controlled by the M23 rebels, says Global WitnessLeading global brands including Amazon, Ericsson and Sony are “likely” to have sourced minerals linked to a militia accused of widespread sexual violence, summary executions and torture, a new investigation claims.The companies allegedly, but unknowingly, acquired coltan smuggled from mines in the Democrati
